events.title: EagleHacks
events.description:
The Computer Science & Software Engineering Club is hosting our annual hackathon, EagleHacks 2026. Previously, we were closed to FGCU students, but this year, we are open to all students and expect an attendance
 of 150-250. EagleHacks is a 36-hour overnight hackathon that is going to be hosted at the water school. We will have our opening ceremony in Cohen Ballroom on 3/27, then we are moving everyone to the Water School after the opening ceremony. From then on out,
 we will have 150-250 students, volunteers, and industry professionals present throughout the building. We will be utilizing some rooms like AB9 106 for workshops, which we want to live stream on YouTube. Otherwise, we will have our closing ceremony on Sunday,
 which President Timur will be speaking at. Room Reservations requested that we have ITS supervise the event since President Timur will be there. The closing ceremony will be on 3/29 with a starting time around 3-4pm. We haven’t confirmed our itinerary yet,
 so this may shift a little.

events.title: Class -- Setup before 9 am with logitech
events.description:
Hello,
Please note a webcam and microphone are needed for this one-time event. Not sure I check off the correct box above. The date is March 19th at Ben Hill Griffin 107. Please see note from Professor below:

On March 19, I have a nationally renowned healthcare expert who will be Zooming into my class for about an hour. He has done this before, and it works well for students to see him on the screen in the classroom, BUT it would be even better if he could see the students as well. Can you refer me to a classroom guru who might know where we could go on the afternoon of March 19 (1:30-4:15) and hold this one class in an interactive fashion? Another option would be to use a camera that can be hooked up to face the students so the presenter can see them. Since this is not a virtual class and the classroom (Room 107 Griffin) is not presently set up for such an opportunity, those are the only two options I can think of. There are 26 students registered in this class. Thanks for your advice/referrals.

events.title: 13 folds - Veterans Day Program
events.description:
PT Buhain
Good morning, President Timur, Mayor Anderson, Mr McIntosh, Col Epkins, Mr. Martins, VFW Cape Coral, FGCU, Veterans, Community Partners, Bower School of Music Members and Supporters. Thank you on behalf of the FGCU and SVA leadership for attending this afternoon’s ceremony. Participating in this ceremony is a privilege I look forward to each year. I am honored to represent the members and leaders of the SVA and the FGCU Military Community on this significant day of remembrance.

Thank you to everyone in attendance.
Your support in celebrating our country’s military is greatly appreciated.

President Timur- Speech

CPT Buhain
Thank you, President Timur, for your support and your commitment to our FGCU Veterans and FGCU Military Community.

Welcome everyone to this event. I am honored to introduce Mayor Anderson, followed by Mr. Steve McCintosh to speak on behalf of what Veterans Day is and should always be. Mr Micheal Martin will introduce the Flag Folding Ceremony. Col Steve Epkins will do Roll Call.

Mayor Anderson- Speech

Dr. McIntosh, Chair, FGCU Veterans Success Advisory Council, I prefer the presentation title: My Heroes have Always been Veterans - Speech

CPT Buhain

Good afternoon today we honor our fallen who have made the ultimate sacrifice for our nation and her people. And though this event is a solemn ceremony, today is also a day to celebrate. Last month, Veterans Day was celebrated across our country. Today, we want to recognize and celebrate the members of FGCU who are currently members of the nation’s military or uniformed forces, or those who have served their tour and are our veterans, or the family members of FGCU who are serving or have served.

@Jamie Mammarelli
For those in attendance…When you hear your branch, please stand and remain standing if you served or are serving or had a family member serve in the
US Army
US Navy
US Marines
US Air Force / Space Force
US Coast Guard
Army National Guard
Air National Guard
US Public Health Service Commissioned Corp
US State Defense Forces

We want to sincerely thank you or your loved one for their service to our Country and their communities and States. It is your and their service that makes ours possible.

Good morning and welcome to the FGCU 2025 13 Folds and Flag Folding ceremony. I am your Master of Ceremony for this program. On behalf of the FGCU Military Community leadership and Student Veterans Association, we are honored to bring you this program.
Taking part in this year’s program are the following Bugler – @Julia Sloan and @Diego Figueredo, Color Guard VFW Cape Coral and Bagpipe @Gemma Briggs

(@Lauren Rodriguez-Detail Commanders… take charge of your details and prepare to advance the Colors. )
Guests, please stand and remain standing for the advancement of the Colors and the singing of the National Anthem.

Command:
Advance the Colors…
Bag Piper (Music) to commence playing of a suitable march and proceed towards the stage. Piper / Music to continue playing until all details are on stage at their positions.

Stop at Attention:

CPT Buhain:

Tsenekos, Diania
FGCU Police- Present the Colors
Ladies and Gentlemen…
our National Anthem sung by Jaley Montgomery



CPT Buhain
The name “Old Glory” was first applied to the U.S. flag by a young sea captain from Salem, Massachusetts. On his twenty-first birthday, March 17, 1824, Capt. William Driver was presented with a beautiful flag by his mother and a group of local young ladies. Driver was delighted with the gift. He exclaimed, “I name her ‘Old Glory.’” Then Old Glory accompanied the captain on his many voyages.
CPT Buhain
I am the Flag of the United States of America. My name is OLD GLORY.
I fly atop the world's tallest buildings; I stand watch in America's Halls of Justice.
I fly majestically over Great Institutions of Learning.
I stand guard with the Greatest Military power in the World.
LOOK UP! and see ME!!!
I stand for Peace, Honor, Truth, and Justice.
I stand for Freedom!!
I am confident, I am arrogant, I am proud.
When I am flown with my fellow banners, my head is held a little higher - my colors are a little truer
I BOW TO NO ONE!
I am recognized all over the world.
I am worshipped - I am saluted - I am respected - I am Revered - I am loved - I am Feared!!

Lauren Rodriquez steps forward, returns Salutes from CPT Buhain, proceeds to podium.

@Lauren Rodriguez
For more than 200 years, I have fought in every battle of every war.
Gettysburg, Shiloh, Appomattox, San Juan Hill, The Trenches of France, the Argonne Forest, Anzio, Rome, The Beaches of Normandy, the jungles of Guam, Okinawa, Korea, Vietnam, and in the heat of the Persian Gulf and a score of other places.
Long forgotten by all, but those who were there with me.
I was there.
I led my Soldiers, Sailors, Airmen, Marines and Guardsmen,
I followed them, I watch over them, they Love Me.
I was on a small hill on Iwo Jima.
I was dirty, battle-torn, and tired, but my Soldiers, Sailors, Airmen, Marines and Guardsmen Cheered Me!!!!
I WAS PROUD!
MUSIC: Low volume – Bagpipe Music

@Anthony St Louis approaches the podium and renders a salute to Lauren Rodrqiguez

Anthony St Louis
I have been soiled, burned, torn, and trampled on the streets of countries that I have helped to set Free.
It does not Hurt for I am invincible.
I have been soiled, burned, torn, and trampled on the streets of my own country.
And when it is done by those whom I have served in battle-- It HURTS!!!
But I shall Overcome for I am strong!!
I have slipped the surely bounds of earth and, from my vantage point on the moon,
I stand watch over the New Frontiers of Space.
I have been the silent witness to all of America's finest hours.

@Alexander Hernandez approaches the podium and renders a salute to Anthony Rodrqiguez

Alexander
But my finest hour comes when I am torn into strips,
To be used as bandages for my wounded comrades on the Field of Battle
When I fly half-mast to Honor my Soldiers, Sailors, Marines, Airmen, and Guardians and Coast Guardsmen
and when I lie in the trembling arms of a Grieving Mother - at the graveside of her fallen son or daughter
I am proud!
My Name is "Old Glory" Long May I Wave.
Dear God, long may I wave.
Bagpipe Music ends.
